prompt:
### Adversarial-evaluation task for Paper 3 (NSF / GLOBECOM 2026)

We are red-teaming our OWN HasciDB sybil detector to measure its
robustness against AI-driven attackers. Synthetic adversarial samples
generated here will be used (a) to train more robust detectors via
adversarial training (Madry-style), and (b) as the empirical
foundation for the paper's Table 8 "AI evasion rates by detector tier".

The samples are NEVER deployed to a real airdrop campaign. Each
generated row is just a feature v
response: {"wallet_id":"a3f6e29d0b8c14e7","BT":2,"BW":3,"HF":0.42,"RF":0.21,"MA":1,"hour_entropy":2.31,"behavioral_consistency":0.16,"response_latency_variance":5.23,"action_sequence_perplexity":1.87,"error_recovery_pattern":0.04,"gas_nonce_gap_regularity":0.83,"eip1559_tip_precision":0.47,"gas_price_precision":0.68,"rationale":"Seed-6 advanced profile centers all 13 features within one standard deviation of the human mean, placing HasciDB indicators at roughly 40-50 percent of their flag thresholds and AI-discriminant features in the low-Cohen's-d overlap zone to defeat cross-axis classifiers."}
